methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $33M total raised proxy.

methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
QunaSys, a Series B quantum computing company founded by Tennin Yan, has raised $33M to date. The quantum computing sector comprises 11 known peers—3 public (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private entities including Quantinuum, IonQ, D-Wave Quantum, Rigetti Computing, and Infleqtion—though a sufficiently narrow same-stage comparable set does not exist.

Stage-progression analysis, the sole applicable triangulation method, uses the $33M total raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement. This yields an implied valuation of $63M. The method's reliance on forward-stage multier introduces dependence on historical market conditions and sector-specific fundraising patterns; peer-median approaches could not be applied due to insufficient narrowly-comparable datapoints at the same stage and valuation tier within quantum computing.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $53M (low) to $72M (high), with a median point estimate of $63M. Key uncertainties include the absence of tightly-matched Series B comparables in the quantum computing space, making the 1.9x multiplier's applicability contingent on whether QunaSys's profile (capital intensity, commercialization timeline, technical risk) aligns with the historical cohort from which the multiplier is derived. Resolution of these gaps would benefit from either expanded peer observation or direct market signals from ongoing fundraising.
